appraisal_events.evidence: « il ne dormait plus, il n'avait plus un moment de calme »; « Fou de jalousie »; et au bordel « on avait prévenu Morel … et placé le pauvre Morel … de telle façon que … voyait en plein le baron. »
appraisal_events.explanation: The narrator stages the baron's jealous dependence, his agitation, and his spying attempt turned ridiculous when the layout of the place exposes his presence to Morel.

appraisal_events.evidence: « Par où baron de Charlus fut protégé d'une infidélité qui le désespérait, et vengé sans l'avoir jamais imaginé, ni surtout comment. »
appraisal_events.explanation: Through an ironic sequence, Morel's fright before the prince permanently deters him from him, which inadvertently protects and avenges the baron.

status_effects.explanation: Locally, he appears diminished and ridiculous due to his jealousy, his spying scheme, and the manipulation he suffered at the brothel.
Favorable ironic outcome: Morel flees the rival and the baron is 'protected and avenged', which improves his emotional position regarding the feared infidelity.
